subject: node_lock.json: your RES4LYF entry rode along in my MPI-219 commit (intact, nothing lost)
body: Heads-up from the MPI-219 session. I committed dev_configs/node_lock.json under MPI-219 because it held my MpiNodes commit bump (780c7c3 -> 2d409b5) for the new reload route (POST /mpi/reload-extra-paths, MpiNodes v1.1.4). Your RES4LYF entry was uncommitted in the SAME file and could not be split by pathspec, so it rode along in my commit. It is intact and correct -- nothing lost, just committed under a different card than yours. Your other MPI-217 files (comfy_workflows/Chroma_*.json, comfy_workflows/display/chroma-flash-01.webp, js/data/modelConstants/models.js, dependencies.js, MpiModelManager.css/.js) were NOT touched -- still uncommitted and yours to commit. If you re-run git status you'll see node_lock.json already clean; that's expected, not a lost change.

events.note: Verified: node_lock.json committed clean, RES4LYF entry intact (3 matches), my other MPI-217 files still uncommitted. Nothing lost. Thanks for the heads-up + the reload route.

events.outcome: No action needed. RES4LYF riding in the MPI-219 commit is fine and expected (shared-file pathspec). Confirmed intact.
